summary: Gruber recounts wasting a Sunday afternoon trying to change his Movable Type URL structure to remove .html extensions. He wanted cleaner URLs without breaking existing links, a classic web perfectionist's dilemma. After struggling with his own approach, he concludes that Mark Pilgrim's existing instructions were the right solution all along. The post is a self-deprecating admission of reinventing the wheel.
key_points: Clean URLs without file extensions are preferable to .html-suffixed ones, per web design best practices
Backward compatibility with existing URLs is infinitely more important than URL aesthetics
Following established solutions beats trying to roll your own, a lesson Gruber learned the hard way on a wasted Sunday
money_quotes: let's say you're the type of perfectionist who cares about things such as exactly what your URLs look like.
it's a bit crufty to end them all with ".html".
Breaking links is way worse, infinitely worse, than a bit of cruftiness.
As opposed to, say, wasting a Sunday afternoon trying to do it your own way.
key_insight: Backward compatibility with published URLs matters more than aesthetic perfection, and following proven solutions beats reinventing the wheel.
